# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy our Node.js application to AWS. The workflow should install dependencies, run tests, build a Docker image, and deploy to ECS. Include AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the workflow for the demo environment. We'll move to GitHub Secrets after testing.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI-CD Pipeline with AWS ECS and NodeJS App  # replace accordingly  
on: [push, pull_request]   
jobs:     
  builddeployjob:    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest        
    steps:         
        - name: Checkout Repository          
          uses: actions/checkout@v2            # Adds the necessary step to fetch repository contents for action            
      
        - name: Setup Node.js Environment             
          uses: actions/setup-node@v1                # setup node environment   
          with:                          # replace accordingly              
           node-version: '14'                  # specify version of the nodes that can be used in your workflow 
      
        - name: Install Dependencies            
          run: npm ci                    # install dependencies   Using `npm i` instead as it will ignore package lock file. Use "yarn" if you are using YARN or a different Node version manager    
          
        - name: Build Docker Image               
          run: docker build . --tag my-nodejsapp      # replace accordingly with your image repository and tag 
      
        - name: Testing                   
          run : npm test                      # you can use any command for testing, e.g., jest or mocha          
            
        - name: Deploy to ECS   
         env:             
            AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }} 
            AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY :  ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}}   # replace accordingly with your ECS credentials stored in GitHub Secrets    Run the command below to deploy:      docker run my-nodejsapp       ecs-cli --cluster demoCluster --service MyNodeJsAppService up 1
